Hi Nicholas,

I hope you're well & having a good week so far?

If you’re still open to it, I’d love to arrange a quick call to discuss our involvement in UK & US university media ownership, and explore how we could support HoYoverse.

Would you have some time later today or early next week?
Zac

Hi Nicholas,

I hope you're well & having a good week so far?

If you're available later today, or at some point this week, I'd love to schedule some time to chat? I'm based in London, so happy to work around your time zone and take an early call!

Please let me know if that works for you,
Zac

Hi Nicholas,
Hope you're well - I appreciate I only emailed a couple days back, however I noticed today that my emails might be hitting your inbox at an inconvenient time

Therefore, I have scheduled this one; hopefully you see it before it gets lost! Would you be free at some point later today? I could do 5pm your time?

Would be great to chat,
Zac

Hi Nicholas,

Hope you're well - did you manage to take a look at my last email?

Would be great to chat if you had some time coming up? I can be as available as you need & happy to chat over Discord if easier

Zac

On Mon, 2 Feb 2026 at 08:03, Zachary Bell <zacharybell@native.fm<mailto:zacharybell@native.fm>> wrote:
Hi Nicholas,

Great to hear from you! Thanks for the quick reply

Native is the largest university media network in the UK, and for most campuses, the media owner.

We work directly with student unions through exclusive agreements, which means we own and run the media on campus. Because of that, everything we do, sits within the university space. Therefore, every case study we have, is in the university space.

For example, If a publisher tried to go straight to universities like Cambridge, Oxford, or Durham to activate on-campus IPs, they’d be sent to us. The same applies if you’re working through an agency - where a university is exclusive (which most are), activity has to run through us.

I'll link our website here<http://native.fm> for more context, but if you're free today to chat - perhaps in 2 hours? I'd love to show you more, especially what we have done in gaming

Zac

On Mon, 2 Feb 2026 at 04:05, Nicchang.C <nicholas.c@hoyoverse.com<mailto:nicholas.c@hoyoverse.com>> wrote:
Hi Zach,

Can you please share us a few past campaign that you have run in the university space? Please include information like list of universities that participated in your campaign, number of signups, and other key information.

That would be helpful for us before we set up the call

Best
Nicholas Chang | Overseas Brand Marketing

Hi Nicholas,

I’d love to connect following your recent LinkedIn post, which I was tagged in and also sent by several people.

I work for Native<http://native.fm>, the largest media network for universities across the UK (& the US). We own and operate the campus media, fair programs, and experiential activations, working directly with universities nationwide.

I lead everything gaming @ Native, & my entire background is working with the largest gaming-focussed agencies, including on campaigns with HoYoverse. Native also has extensive experience in this space, partnering with publishers such as Capcom and Tencent, to bring their IPs onto campus through engaging student-focused activities.

If you have availability next week, I’d welcome the opportunity to connect and explore whether there may be potential alignment - let me know when you can chat!

Zac

subject: Email
body_text:
Good morning ,

My name is Aaron and I manage the events and promotion of all Comedy Club and experiences to students across the UK.

We are currently partnered with over 75 universities across the UK and are the trusted media partner for their Students' Unions.

I wanted to contact you regarding our upcoming opportunities across this year that can help support you with engaging across the student population per University, region or across the UK.

We run a wide range of monthly events at key high footfall areas on-campus and digitally to increase awareness and promotional campaigns. This also includes all September and October Freshers’ Fairs that remain the unrivalled way of targeting students face to face.

If this is strikes a chord with your wider marketing strategy that will help reach your conversion targets, then please get in touch as I would be delighted to discuss a wide range of opportunities that could drive engagement effectively.

subject: Re: How RIXO could reach 2.9M Gen Z
body_text:
Hello Emma,
I hope you are well! I wanted to check in to see if you have any thoughts on my previous emails?
Kind regards,
Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 00:26, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Emma,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 05:22, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Emma,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

On February 24, 2026 02:17, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Karen,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 05:44, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Karen,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

subject: Re: How RIXO could reach 2.9M Gen Z
body_text:
Hello Lydia,
I hope you are well! I wanted to check in to see if you have any thoughts on my previous emails?
Kind regards,
Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 01:56, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Lydia,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 05:39, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Lydia,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

subject: Re: How RIXO could reach 2.9M Gen Z
body_text:
Hello Rosalee,
I hope you are well! I wanted to check in to see if you have any thoughts on my previous emails?
Kind regards,
Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 02:12, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Rosalee,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 05:28, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Rosalee,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

subject: Re: How RIXO could reach 2.9M Gen Z
body_text:
Hello Josie,
I hope you are well! I wanted to check in to see if you have any thoughts on my previous emails?
Kind regards,
Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 00:49, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Josie,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 05:33, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Josie,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

body_text:
Hello Lauren,
I hope you are well! I wanted to check in to see if you have any thoughts on my previous emails?
Kind regards,
Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 02:01, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Lauren,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 06:12, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Lauren,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

Hello Paul,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.

Hi Jutta,

Thanks for getting some of the details across. Please send any outstanding
items over in the next day or so, we need to get this to the venue well in
advance of the event starting.

Also see further info on the two points you queried...


   - *PLI Certificate* - This is your Public Liability Certificate, its an
   insurance policy that covers you in case of any extreme circumstances that
   may happen at an event. We just need a copy attached to an email.
   - *Risk Assessment *- A risk assessment is a very topline breakdown that
   highlights any potential risks the public or your staff may be exposed to
   at the event. I have attached a template of a RAMs form, there are a few
   examples in this to help you complete it. This should cover any incidents
   that are major or minor.



Is there also any update from you on the larger gamified set up? I know
this week will be a bus one for you with traveling to the UK for the BSLI.
Could we get any update from you today/tomorrow so we can reshape plans
accordingly with a view of having a revised proposal with you next week
when you are back in?

--

Mark Hodge (he/him)

Business Development Manager

The fairs are themed and generally take place once a month while on campus.


The upcoming themes of the fairs are 'Sports and Fitness' (March), 'Health and Wellness' (April) and 'Set Up Your Summer' (May).


I think we could find a connection to you for each of the fairs (e.g. if there was a protein focus for the sports fair), Health and Wellness may be a bit more of a stretch, but Set Up Your Summer is pretty generic.

In terms of organising something with the landlord - we have the space to fill spaces at the pre-organised events, but this would give you an opportunity to rub shoulders with the SU team, who may agree to organise a separate event if you can get in with them, but at the moment it's a bit outside of our scope.

You can provide a street-food pop up that's successfully been run at a number of locations, including University of Herts. Your gazebos are 3x3m and you can get a variety of food vendors in, you just require the space and permissions from the university.


Just to flag we may run into a couple of issues - namely universities wanting to protect their revenue from their on-site catering, and so not allowing third parties to sell food on site, and also that we at native are only really responsible for the student fairs, and so it may be difficult to organise you a separate pop-up outside of these.


However, I'll do some digging into individual universities and see if we can get a positive response on both points, and come back to you with some options.


Once we've got the greenlight, we can also arrange a time for you to visit the space and research waste sites, etc.


Thanks, and will be back shortly,


Jack

Ashton Rose (she/her)

Powering campus culture


Original Thread:
No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 24, 2026 00:31, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:

Hello Lillian,


Students are surrounded by fast fashion options promising trend led pieces at speed.


But there is a growing appetite for something more considered, pieces with character, longevity and a sense of individuality. The challenge is helping that mindset translate into real brand discovery.


At native, we place brands within trusted student environments, both physically on campus and digitally through targeted channels students actively engage with. It is not broad social exposure. It is credible presence where conversations and recommendations feel natural.


For RIXO, that could mean aligning with key student milestones and showcasing how a single piece can carry from university formal to post graduation events. When one dress becomes “the dress” within a friendship group, momentum builds organically.


Would it be useful to explore how this could support RIXO’s continued growth with Gen Z consumers?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture
  

No longer interested in these messages? Unsubscribe
On February 19, 2026 06:00, Ashton Rose <ashtonrose@native.fm> wrote:


Hello Lillian,


University is filled with moments that call for something special.


Formals, graduations, summer balls, weddings, city breaks. It is often the first time young women are investing in pieces they want to wear more than once, not just for one night but for years to come.


That is where RIXO fits beautifully.


When a student discovers a brand that feels distinctive, flattering and timeless, it often becomes their go to for every meaningful event that follows. If that connection forms early, it can carry well beyond graduation.


Through native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students via on campus activations, embedded brand ambassadors and targeted digital placements that build visibility within highly social campus networks.


Would you be open to a short conversation about positioning RIXO at the heart of student occasion dressing across the UK?


Kind regards,


  
  Ashton Rose (she/her)
Sales Development Representative 


Powering campus culture

to talk on Friday, it was great to meet you both.

on the call this may change in the summer with some new additions on the
horizon, nonetheless we can manage those when they occur.

rough scan and from what I can see you have booked 91/112 spaces, leaving

historical discrepancy which needs to be factored in.

Hi Dave,

subject: Leeds Rhinos Foundation - Sports & Fitness Fair
body_text:
Hey Leeds Rhinos Foundation, 



I’m Neil, UK Booking Manager for Charities & Non-Profit Organisations at Native. We’re the official media partner to 50+ Students’ Unions nationwide, helping organisations engage directly with students on campus. 



On 10th March at the University of Leeds, we’re hosting a student engagement fair focused on Sports & Fitness. It feels like a great fit for you to come along and connect face-to-face with the student population. 



We’re expecting over 1,900 students across the day. 



I appreciate this is a little last minute. We would normally charge for stalls at these events, but given the timescales, if you’re able to attend I’m happy to offer the space free of charge on this occasion. 



If you’d like the opportunity to engage with students in Leeds and tell them more about the Foundation, just let me know and I can share the details. 



Many thanks, 

Neil Eggar

subject: Re: Reaching London Uni Students
body_text:
Hey Nicole, 



Thanks for coming back - have answered your questions below. 



Yes, there are costs associated for the vendor. Our biggest events are Freshers, and the costs for these vary per site based mainly on the number of attendees. 



The prices here start at £450 + VAT per site, and go up to £1500 + VAT. 



We also have events that run all the way through the year - including two Entertainment Fairs (one in November, and one in February). The purpose of these fairs are to introduce students to cool stuff to do in their spare time - so would be a good fit for 99 Club Comedy. 



Prices here start at £250 + VAT and go up to £770 + VAT. 



We're also currently running a promotion for the remaining fairs happening in London up until September. When buying a stall at Freshers, you can get one of the fairs inthis spreadsheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ekAW8wuxCClC6bzQnpU5S0iT-WbZwOByHs4g2LRfsiw/edit?gid=0#gid=0 (so for example, you could attend the Set Up Your Summer and Freshers Fair at London South Bank for £700 + VAT). 



These are 'Health and Wellness' (where we could use the angle of a good laugh making everyone feel better), and the 'Set Up Your Summer' Fairs where we introduce students to cool stuff before they break up for the summer. 



And if you wanted a quick call to talk any of this through, let me know or book 15 minutes with me at the button below. 



Thanks,

Hi Stefan,

Great to hear from you, glad you’re well!

Good to hear things are going well with Liv and Calum! We actually caught up with the team at EA recently too, lovely bunch.

In terms of our remit, yes, that absolutely sits with us.

Long story short, Native<https://d2y6r004.eu1.hs-sales-engage.com/Ctc/RJ+23284/d2Y6r004/Jks2-6qcW69sMD-6lZ3njW3GdmBH3W1n_XVsSbNF5ZM-z7W4vQMLx831KzTW7jv2nZ3Rc0vnW3XKZT85v4Q6zVj0Yr08fKQBBW2PsqQS3SZ8DQW55mCRz3p9c72W6SGqv19gX39cW5BJ6JM8GPC3XN2QX_kKJcNX0V_ZlXC81XqXcMy9dq6WGqS0W7-jY5G8hXzBdW4ssVhc1F8L0BW7Q5SWS16SL5_W6f78724bFzVSW1Ml4-T3491GWW4kkNnC1Zv0WQW4WPrr44W1lhBf4kmgQz04> is the leading media network for universities and student unions across the UK (& soon, the US). In the UK, we partner exclusively with most universities and work with all of them. Through those exclusive partnerships, we own the media. Freshers fairs, bespoke campus events, email marketing, social amplification etc, all run through us, even if you choose to activate via an agency.

I have actually put together a deck that’s more gaming-focused, to the point, that also includes a short case study from our latest work with Capcom. We’re working with them again in March, so I’ll be able to build that out further. I’m also pulling together a case study from our work with Tencent, which I can share once finalised!

Would be great to reconnect if you’ve got some time next week. Happy to jump on a video call or grab a coffee or lunch, whatever works best!

Zac

Liv’s just left to have a baby and Calum seems to be doing well at EA,

Cheers,
Stef

subject: Leeds United Foundation - Sports & Fitness Fair
body_text:
Hey Leeds United Foundation, 



I’m Neil, UK Booking Manager for Charities & Non-Profit Organisations at Native. We’re the official media partner to 50+ Students’ Unions nationwide, helping organisations engage directly with students on campus. 



On 10th March at the University of Leeds, we’re hosting a student engagement fair focused on Sports & Fitness. It feels like a great fit for you to come along and connect face-to-face with the student population. 



We’re expecting over 1,900 students across the day. 



I appreciate this is a little last minute. We would normally charge for stalls at these events, but given the timescales, if you’re able to attend I’m happy to offer the space free of charge on this occasion. 



If you’d like the opportunity to engage with students in Leeds and tell them more about the Foundation, just let me know and I can share the details. 



Many thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Powering campus culture

subject: Action for Sport - Sports & Fitness Fair
body_text:
Hey Action for Sport Team, 



I’m Neil, UK Booking Manager for Charities & Non-Profit Organisations at Native. We’re the official media partner to 50+ Students’ Unions nationwide, helping organisations engage directly with students on campus. 



On 10th March at the University of Leeds, we’re hosting a student engagement fair focused on Sports & Fitness. It feels like a great fit for you to come along and connect face-to-face with the student population. 



We’re expecting over 1,900 students across the day. 



I appreciate this is a little last minute. We would normally charge for stalls at these events, but given the timescales, if you’re able to attend I’m happy to offer the space free of charge on this occasion. 



If you’d like the opportunity to engage with students in Leeds and tell them more about the Foundation, just let me know and I can share the details. 



Many thanks, 

Neil Eggar --

We partner with universities across London to host some of the UK’s biggest Freshers Fairs every September. These events give production companies, ticketing platforms, venues, and organisations in the performing arts space a chance to connect directly with thousands of enthusiastic Gen Z students who are starting their first experience living, working, and studying in London.

Is university Gen Z part of your target audience?
Have you found it challenging to reach this often hard-to-access group?

We partner with universities across London to host some of the UK’s biggest Freshers Fairs every September. Is university Gen Z part of your target audience?

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Reina,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

-- 

Alex Stoffel

Brand Partnerships

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Dipesh,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Emma,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

-- 

Alex Stoffel

subject: Promoting to London Students - Freshers 2026
body_text:
Hi Fred,

We partner with universities across London to host some of the UK’s biggest Freshers Fairs every September. These events give production companies, ticketing platforms, venues, and organisations in the performing arts space a chance to connect directly with thousands of enthusiastic Gen Z students who are starting their first experience living, working, and studying in London.

Is university Gen Z part of your target audience?
Have you found it challenging to reach this often hard-to-access group?

I’d be delighted to schedule: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/matt-billington a short call to introduce our events, campus fairs, and digital advertising opportunities this year — and explore how we can help you engage university students near you effectively.

I look forwards to connecting!
Matt Billington (he/him)

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Anjali,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

-- 

Alex Stoffel

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Anjali,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

-- 

Alex Stoffel

Brand Partnerships

subject: Promoting to London Students - Freshers 2026
body_text:
Hi Jerry,

We partner with universities across London to host some of the UK’s biggest Freshers Fairs every September. These events give production companies, ticketing platforms, venues, and organisations in the performing arts space a chance to connect directly with thousands of enthusiastic Gen Z students who are starting their first experience living, working, and studying in London.

subject: Reaching students on campus with Tesco Mobile
body_text:
Hi Holly,

University is often when students choose their own mobile plan - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

Tesco Mobile already fits how value-conscious Gen Z stays connected -
affordable plans, flexible options, and a trusted household brand. The
opportunity is showing up while those mobile habits and provider loyalties
are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Alex

subject: Re: Welcome Back & Play Your Way fairs / Adobe
body_text:
Hi Annette,

Hope you're well too and apologies for the delayed reply from Friday; I
hope you had a lovely weekend.

*Expected Footfall & Student Breakdown*

   - We are aiming for 8,000 registrations; however, we do expect some
   drop-off with free events.
   - I have pasted below the full list of percentages per school:

*University / Organisation Percentage (%)*
Union of Brunel Students,16.73%
Students' Union UCL,10.13%
London Met Student's Union,10.05%
Queen Mary University Students Union,6.07%
Westminster Students' Union,6.08%
LSE STUDENTS' UNION,6.87%
South Bank Students' Union,3.89%
University of West London Students' Union,3.90%
University of Goldsmiths Student Union,2.91%
City St George's Students' Union,2.47%
Brighton Students' Union,2.34%
Arts Students' Union,1.98%
Roehampton Students' Union,1.98%
Middlesex Students’ Union,1.70%
Aston Students' Union,1.70%
SOAS Students Union,1.63%
Anglia Ruskin SU,1.35%
Birkbeck Students' Union,1.35%
University of Greenwich,1.20%
Coventry University 0.92%

*Pre Promo*

   - I have requested screenshots and will send them as soon as I have
   them.
   - If we can have this as soon as possible, we are doing some mailouts
   this week and a big push ahead of the event on Saturday. I am confirming
   with the team and will get back to you with timelines and dates.


   - A first party send is essentially a first party solus, apologies for
   any confusion here. They are essentially the same thing, the first party is
   our database.
   - Since we already regularly promote the BSLI to our ticket holders,
   including Adobe's logo, a solus targeting only ticket holders isn't really
   worth doing. If you wanted to do a solus, I'd recommend using our London
   database for a first-party solus. This allows you to promote to a wider
   audience, including students who haven't registered for a ticket yet and
   may require an additional nudge to drive day-of attendance. I will send
   some across some further details on this.
   - Noted on HTML, I'll see if there's anything we can do on our side to
   help.

Best,
Ana

annette@seedmarketingagency.com> wrote:

> Hi Ana,
>
> Hope you’re doing well & happy Friday! Thanks so much for getting back to
> us, really appreciate it.
>
> Just a few more Qs if that's ok!
>
> Expected Footfall & Student Breakdown
>
>    -
>
>    Thanks so much for sharing the breakdown - could you confirm that the
>    expected footfall is still 8k based on ticket sales?
>    -
>
>    Would it be possible to get a breakdown of students per school as
>    attendees don’t need to come from a Creative Campus they’ll still receive 6
>    months of free premium with an account.
>
>
> Pre Promo
>
>    -
>
>    Could you share any pre-promo emails that have already gone out? We’d
>    like to share these with Adobe.
>    -
>
>    I’ll ask Adobe re imagery and copy. Is there a deadline for these, and
>    when is the email scheduled to go out?
>
>
>
>    -
>
>    Sorry, could you clarify the difference between a first-party send and
>    a solus send?
>    -
>
>    Is it possible to only send to ticket holders only just as I know cost
>    is done per send
>
>
>    -
>
>    I’ll also check feasibility on the HTML - not sure if they’ll have
>    enough time to do this in house for the solus but will keep you posted.
>
>
> Thanks so much!
>
> Best,
> Annette
>
>
> On Wed, 25 Feb 2026 at 16:35, Ana Grant <anagrant@native.fm> wrote:
>
>> Hey Annette,
>>
>> Hope you're well! I am just jumping in here and coming back to you on
>> your questions around the Big Student Lock In.
>>
>> *Would it be possible to get a breakdown of the expected number as well
>> as % of students attending per school based on ticket sales?*
>> I’ve included a breakdown below showing the percentage of registered
>> students attending per Students’ Union across your creative campuses. I’m
>> also working on pulling expected volumes and will share those as soon as
>> possible, but wanted to provide this view in the meantime.
>>
>> If there are any additional London Students’ Unions now included in the
>> Creative Campus agreement, please let me know and I can update the
>> breakdown. Please note, these figures are based on registered students who
>> have provided their location details and a verified university email
>> address (e.g. ending in ac.uk).
>>
>>    - University of West London Students' Union,3.90%
>>    - University of Goldsmiths Student Union,2.91%
>>    - Arts Students' Union,1.98%
>>    - Middlesex Students’ Union,1.70%
>>    - Aston Students' Union,1.70%
>>    - Anglia Ruskin SU,1.35%
>>    - London Met Student's Union,10.05%
>>
>> *Will our logo be included in the general email send, and is there
>> anything specific you need from us for that?*
>>
>>    - We have been doing some pre promotion already using different
>>    platforms and I can provide some more detail on this if needed. But
>>    specifically using the Adobe logo, across our pre-promotion emails, we have

subject: Promoting to London Students - Freshers 2026
body_text:
Hi Ian,

We partner with universities across London to host some of the UK’s biggest Freshers Fairs every September. These events give production companies, ticketing platforms, venues, and organisations in the performing arts space a chance to connect directly with thousands of enthusiastic Gen Z students who are starting their first experience living, working, and studying in London.

Is university Gen Z part of your target audience?
Have you found it challenging to reach this often hard-to-access group?

I’d be delighted to schedule: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/matt-billington a short call to introduce our events, campus fairs, and digital advertising opportunities this year — and explore how we can help you engage university students near you effectively.

I look forwards to connecting!
Matt Billington (he/him)

Hi Linoy,
I’ve reached out a few times to talk about how we can help Playtika Holding connect with the incredible student audience across the UK, and I’m starting to wonder if one of these is happening:

You’ve already nailed your Gen Z and student marketing strategy for 2025 (in that case, high five!).
You’re super interested but buried under a ton of other things, and just haven’t had time to reply.
You’ve taken a little tumble and can’t get up—if that’s the case, let me know and I’ll send help!

I’d love to hear your thoughts over a quick call and figure out how we can make a big splash with students this year!
Let's Make it Happen!Kind RegardsSheraaz Ahmed

The biggest student event of the year our Big Student Lock-In  is where that happens. 


10,000+ students under one roof, sharing experiences, creating trends, and spreading the buzz online. This isn’t just an event - it’s a stage for your brand to be seen, remembered, and loved.

subject: Yorkshire Sports Foundation - University of Leeds Sports & Fitness Fair
body_text:
Hey Yorkshire Sport Foundation, 



I’m Neil, UK Booking Manager for Charities & Non-Profit Organisations at Native. We’re the official media partner to 50+ Students’ Unions nationwide, helping organisations engage directly with students on campus. 



On 10th March at the University of Leeds, we’re hosting a student engagement fair focused on Sports & Fitness. It feels like a great fit for you to come along and connect face-to-face with the student population. 



We’re expecting over 1,900 students across the day. 



The event is being held at, Lifton Place in Union Square 



I appreciate this is a little last minute. We would normally charge for stalls at these events, but given the timescales, if you’re able to attend I’m happy to offer the space free of charge on this occasion. 



If you’d like the opportunity to engage with students in Leeds and tell them more about the Foundation, just let me know and I can share the details. 



Many thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Tel: 01224 980141


Powering campus culture

subject: Follow Up on Sprayd University Fairs Booking
body_text:
Hi Charlie, 



I hope you're well. I wanted to send a quick courtesy email as I have followed up a few times to check whether the booking form reached you, but I have not received a reply. This is unusual based on our previous conversations, so I am wondering if there has been a change in interest regarding the Sprayd brand activation at the university summer fairs. 



I also tried to leave a voicemail today to update you with the below, but the call appeared to cut off partway through. We now have the dates and rates confirmed for some of our larger Freshers’ Fairs in September. We are offering all of our summer fairs free of charge to any brand attending Freshers 2026 in September. This gives businesses the chance to attend multiple events for the price of one central booking. 



As I have not heard back, I will close this ticket for now. However, if you would like to revisit student engagement opportunities in the future, I would be very happy to reconnect. The updated Freshers’ policy and free event offer provides a hugely greater presence for only a small additional cost. 



Best 

Matt. 



Matt Billington (he/him) 
University Bookings Manager, Media & Events

subject: Secure Freshers 2026 at University of Leeds – Early Rate Ending Soon
body_text:
Good morning Olympia, 



Thank you again for your time this morning. It was great to speak with you. 



As mentioned, we’re currently taking bookings for Freshers 2026 at the University of Leeds, taking place this September. This is the largest student-facing event of the year, welcoming over 12,000 students per day, all actively looking for places to socialise, eat, drink and spend as they begin term. 



Freshers represent a huge opportunity because: 



Students form habits in Week 1 — the venues they try first often become their go-to for the next 3+ years. 

It drives immediate footfall — exclusive offers and activations convert directly into bookings that same week. 

It builds long-term brand awareness — especially important for venues slightly outside the city-centre “default” locations. 

As BOX Headingley is based in LS6, Freshers is particularly valuable. Many student bars and restaurants in LS1 and LS2 are already well known to new students and, in some cases, have restrictions on-campus promotion. Freshers provides a rare and powerful opportunity to position BOX Headingley directly in front of students before they’ve decided where they’ll spend their time — helping you compete early and drive traffic beyond the city centre. 



We currently have a small window to secure your Freshers 2026 booking at last year’s rate before prices increase (please see Slide 12 for 1-day attendance pricing). 



In addition, I’d love to offer you a chance to trial engagement ahead of September. We can book you into our Feel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness Edition on 10th March with a complimentary Activate Lite spot. This event is expected to attract around 1,900 students, and with standard packages starting from £1,000 + VAT, it’s an excellent opportunity to test messaging, capture data and build early momentum at no cost. (Please see Slide 8 for details.) 



I’ve also included Slide 13, which outlines the potential revenue impact Freshers could generate for BOX Headingley based on average student spend and repeat visits. 



Here is the University of Leeds upcoming fair presentation: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1V-efMGHaMgDBbezn-gypN8BRYR0_Mcjbdq2J7qhCu3A/edit?usp=sharing 



If this is of interest, I’d recommend securing the Feel Good Fair space as soon as possible while availability remains. 



Please let me know if you have any questions. I’d be more than happy to help. 



Kind regards, 
Sat Singh 



Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir

subject: Secure Freshers 2026 at University of Leeds – Early Rate Ending Soon
body_text:
Good morning Olympia, 



Thank you again for your time this morning. It was great to speak with you. 



As mentioned, we’re currently taking bookings for Freshers 2026 at the University of Leeds, taking place this September. This is the largest student-facing event of the year, welcoming over 12,000 students per day, all actively looking for places to socialise, eat, drink and spend as they begin term. 



Freshers represent a huge opportunity because: 



Students form habits in Week 1 — the venues they try first often become their go-to for the next 3+ years. 

It drives immediate footfall — exclusive offers and activations convert directly into bookings that same week. 

It builds long-term brand awareness — especially important for venues slightly outside the city-centre “default” locations. 

As BOX Headingley is based in LS6, Freshers is particularly valuable. Many student bars and restaurants in LS1 and LS2 are already well known to new students and, in some cases, have restrictions on-campus promotion. Freshers provides a rare and powerful opportunity to position BOX Headingley directly in front of students before they’ve decided where they’ll spend their time — helping you compete early and drive traffic beyond the city centre. 



We currently have a small window to secure your Freshers 2026 booking at last year’s rate before prices increase (please see Slide 12 for 1-day attendance pricing). 



In addition, I’d love to offer you a chance to trial engagement ahead of September. We can book you into our Feel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness Edition on 10th March with a complimentary Activate Lite spot. This event is expected to attract around 1,900 students, and with standard packages starting from £1,000 + VAT, it’s an excellent opportunity to test messaging, capture data and build early momentum at no cost. (Please see Slide 8 for details.) 



I’ve also included Slide 13, which outlines the potential revenue impact Freshers could generate for BOX Headingley based on average student spend and repeat visits. 



Here is the University of Leeds upcoming fair presentation: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1V-efMGHaMgDBbezn-gypN8BRYR0_Mcjbdq2J7qhCu3A/edit?usp=sharing 



If this is of interest, I’d recommend securing the Feel Good Fair space as soon as possible while availability remains. 



Please let me know if you have any questions. I’d be more than happy to help. 



Kind regards, 
Sat Singh Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir

subject: Reaching students on campus with giffgaff
body_text:
Hi Jolisa,

Students often choose their own mobile plan during university - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

giffgaff already fits how digitally confident Gen Z stays connected -
flexible, contract-free plans with a community-driven model that feels
independent and straightforward. The opportunity is showing up while those
mobile habits and provider loyalties are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Best,
Alex

subject: Secure Freshers 2026 at University of Leeds – Early Rate Ending Soon
body_text:
Good morning Olympia,

Thank you again for your time this morning. It was great to speak with you.

As mentioned, we’re currently taking bookings for Freshers 2026 at the University of Leeds, taking place this September. This is the largest student-facing event of the year, welcoming over 12,000 students per day, all actively looking for places to socialise, eat, drink and spend as they begin term.

Freshers represent a huge opportunity because:


  *
Students form habits in Week 1 — the venues they try first often become their go-to for the next 3+ years.
  *
It drives immediate footfall — exclusive offers and activations convert directly into bookings that same week.
  *
It builds long-term brand awareness — especially important for venues slightly outside the city-centre “default” locations.

As BOX Headingley is based in LS6, Freshers is particularly valuable. Many student bars and restaurants in LS1 and LS2 are already well known to new students and, in some cases, have restrictions on-campus promotion. Freshers provides a rare and powerful opportunity to position BOX Headingley directly in front of students before they’ve decided where they’ll spend their time — helping you compete early and drive traffic beyond the city centre.

We currently have a small window to secure your Freshers 2026 booking at last year’s rate before prices increase (please see Slide 12 for 1-day attendance pricing).

In addition, I’d love to offer you a chance to trial engagement ahead of September. We can book you into our Feel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness Edition on 10th March with a complimentary Activate Lite spot. This event is expected to attract around 1,900 students, and with standard packages starting from £1,000 + VAT, it’s an excellent opportunity to test messaging, capture data and build early momentum at no cost. (Please see Slide 8 for details.)

I’ve also included Slide 13, which outlines the potential revenue impact Freshers could generate for BOX Headingley based on average student spend and repeat visits.

Here is the University of Leeds upcoming fair presentation<https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1V-efMGHaMgDBbezn-gypN8BRYR0_Mcjbdq2J7qhCu3A/edit?usp=sharing>

If this is of interest, I’d recommend securing the Feel Good Fair space as soon as possible while availability remains.

Please let me know if you have any questions. I’d be more than happy to help.

Kind regards,
Sat Singh

Hello Sat,

Thanks very much.

I’ll wait to hear from you - the expectations that were set were the worst
part, if that had been in line I’d have made different decisions. I hired
movers to move machines to and from the event and I hired a photographer -
this whole endeavour cost me just under £1k for not even a single sign up.
Not including lost revenue in closing the studio.

If I had known the footfall would be nowhere near the 1400 advertised - I’d
have brought a mat and not bothered with photography.

Even the staff within the ‘Engine Shed’ said it’s usually very quiet - not
sure why it was the best place to exhibit. Students had no idea we were
there, the event was not well communicated.

Warmly,
Joyce

Students often choose their own mobile plan during university - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

giffgaff already fits how digitally confident Gen Z stays connected -
flexible, contract-free plans with a community-driven model that feels
independent and straightforward. The opportunity is showing up while those
mobile habits and provider loyalties are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping telecom brands build early familiarity and
long-term engagement.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?

Students often choose their own mobile plan during university - managing
data, budgeting monthly costs, and relying on strong connectivity for
study, streaming, and social life.

giffgaff already fits how digitally confident Gen Z stays connected -

  1.
Risk Assessment

Please provide a comprehensive Risk Assessment covering your activities at the fair. We've attached a basic template should you need it but you can use your own version if you have one.

Even for a simple activation, the form must be filled out to ensure all parties are covered in case anything happens. The assessment should cover things like avoiding trip hazards, controlling crowds and queues near your stall, electrical hazards, etc

For more complex activations, the Risk Assessment should cover vehicle safety, weather proofing (if applicable), sampling including any risks around food distribution such as allergens or food prep and general health and safety hazards around your stall.


Other Information Required

In addition to the documentation, please provide the following:


  *
Power Socket Requirements - should you require power to run your activation, please let us know. Note that some fairs can only provide a limited number of power sockets so we cannot always guarantee access. The sooner you let us know, the better.
  *
Vehicle details - please provide the registration number of any vehicles which will be a) used to load and unload your kit and b) will need to park
  *
Names of attendees and contact number - we will also need the names of the individuals who will be attending the fair along with at least one contact number
  *
Description of activity - please provide a brief description of your set up and any sampling/giveaways which will be part of your activation


Roller Banner

As part of your booking we can print a roller banner for you. The price of this is included in your booking fee, and so we can print and send this to you, provided that you send over the assets for us in time.

To submit your roller banner design, please open the attached PDF document.

You’ll have two options:


  1.
Submit your own design

If you already have your own banner artwork, please follow the design guidelines listed on page 2 of the PDF. This includes the required specs and file format. You’ll be able to upload your design directly through the form.

  1.
Use one of our templates

If you’d like us to create your banner, go to page 3 of the PDF and choose one of the five design templates. Click the “Use Template” button under your preferred design and follow the instructions to submit:

  *
A high-res PNG of your company logo
  *
Your chosen banner copy (title and subtitle)
  *
Optionally: a QR code, social links, and a feature image (depending on the template)

Deadline: Artwork must be submitted at least 10 working days before your fair date

Please also remember to provide your delivery address when submitting, so we can send your banner directly to you.


If you have any questions, of course let us know.

Many thanks,

—
Afam Okafor

subject: Re: The Biggest Student Event of The Year.
body_text:
Hi Christian,
I’ve reached out a few times to talk about how we can help Scopely connect with the incredible student audience across the UK, and I’m starting to wonder if one of these is happening:

You’ve already nailed your Gen Z and student marketing strategy for 2025 (in that case, high five!).
You’re super interested but buried under a ton of other things, and just haven’t had time to reply.
You’ve taken a little tumble and can’t get up—if that’s the case, let me know and I’ll send help!

I’d love to hear your thoughts over a quick call and figure out how we can make a big splash with students this year!
Let's Make it Happen!Kind RegardsSheraaz Ahmed


Sheraaz Ahmed (he/him)

If students were choosing their favourite brands today - would yours make it into their Instagram stories, Snapchat selfies, and WhatsApp group chats? 

The biggest student event of the year our Big Student Lock-In  is where that happens. 


10,000+ students under one roof, sharing experiences, creating trends, and spreading the buzz online. This isn’t just an event - it’s a stage for your brand to be seen, remembered, and loved.

Who doesn’t want thousands of students talking about your brand online?

Could we arrange a quick call this week or early next to explore how your brand can make a significant impact on students and become one they carry with them long after they graduate?

subject: Re: The Biggest Student Event of The Year.
body_text:
Hi Talia,
I’ve reached out a few times to talk about how we can help Scopely connect with the incredible student audience across the UK, and I’m starting to wonder if one of these is happening:

You’ve already nailed your Gen Z and student marketing strategy for 2025 (in that case, high five!).
You’re super interested but buried under a ton of other things, and just haven’t had time to reply.
You’ve taken a little tumble and can’t get up—if that’s the case, let me know and I’ll send help!

I’d love to hear your thoughts over a quick call and figure out how we can make a big splash with students this year!
Let's Make it Happen!Kind RegardsSheraaz Ahmed


Sheraaz Ahmed (he/him)

Powering campus culture

subject: RE: The Big Student Lock In - Business Design Centre - 7th March
body_text:
Hi Blessy, 



Just sharing some information about the Big Student Lock In, where we will bring 8000 students together at the Business Design Centre in Islington. 



Beyond this, we regularly hold events at universities in London, so if this one is too soon and there is not enough time to organise, it may be worth us having a meeting to talk you through different ways we can market to students across the year. 



Big Student Lock In 

Saturday 7th March 

10:00 - 16:00 

8000 Students 

We've partnered with 12 London universities to bring students from across London together, which would give you the chance to go and represent your business and get more students into Chilango. 



The cost for a standard stall here is £1000 + VAT - which will include your table, tablecloth, wifi and electricity, and a branded roller banner for Chilango. We've reduced this from £1500 + VAT as it's last minute, and we're trying to close the remaining stalls. 



You can see the video from last year's event here: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/nativecampus_thebigstudentlockin-studentmarketing-brandexperience-activity-7391765966327816192-xMvE?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAADLP3SMB0Jppx1FrXN5P8jJjwGtF2JECa6w, and I've attached the media pack for you to review. 



If you want to discuss this with your management and come back, that would be brilliant.

Hi Alex,

Following up on my note from last week regarding student awareness,
engagement, and acquisition.

We’re helping social and dating platforms like Bumble build early
preference with UK students through on-campus activations and society
partnerships - right as relationship habits, social discovery behaviours,
and long-term app loyalties are being formed.

Would you be open to a quick chat this or next week to explore?

Best regards,
Alex

I emailed you back on 15 December, drawing to your attention back then that, following a challenge by the Christian Institute, Native had suspended its policy on refusing bookings from religious organisations.

As more recent, relevant background, I wish to bring to your attention the fact that after another of our branches had problems making a booking, Sam Webster of the Christian Institute contacted your colleague Claire Scott-Priestley (Native's General Counsel) last month on behalf of that branch.  On 2 February she advised the Christian Institute in writing that:

  1.
 The refusal to accept that branch's booking was an oversight
  2.
 She confirmed that Native had withdrawn its previous policy and that Good News for Everyone! should be able to make bookings
  3.
She had liaised with appropriate management within Native and could confirm that additional training was being delivered internally to ensure these issues were no longer encountered by our charity.

However, we are now over a fortnight beyond that email from her. The absence of an update from yourself suggests there may still be an ongoing communication problem within Native regarding the change of policy, despite its oversight being brought to its attention over a fortnight ago.

Ian Nevison

Hi The Sheepscar Bar and Gril, 



I hope you’re well. 



We’ve had a cancellation for theFeel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness – 10th March 2026 | Location: The University of Leeds | Lifton Place: Union Square & Precinct - Union Square, Zone A | Start time: 11 am-5 pm: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qMgQIQmLNTuF9vOJjxmP5NUqRH9UeRnM/view?usp=sharing 



Since the space is now vacant, I can offer you a one-time offer for FREE. This is intended to give you the experience of the fair firsthand.

Package: This is an "Activate Lite" booking, which includes a table and a basic activation space (roller banners are not included). 



The Goal: It’s a brilliant way to test the waters, collect student feedback, and prep your strategy to drive even higher engagement at our future fairs. 

Because I can only offer this to one business, please let me know as soon as possible if you’d like to snap it up. I expect the space to be filled within the next hour. 



Kind regards, 



Sat Singh

subject: Catch Leeds - University of Leeds Sports & Fitness Fair
body_text:
Hey Ebony, 



Thank you for taking the time to have a quick chat with me this morning. 



On 10th March at the University of Leeds, we’re hosting a student engagement fair focused on Sports & Fitness. It feels like a great fit for you to come along and connect face-to-face with the student population. 



We’re expecting over 1,900 students across the day. 



The event is being held at, Lifton Place in Union Square 



I appreciate this is a little last minute. We would normally charge for stalls at these events, but given the timescales, if you’re able to attend I’m happy to offer the space free of charge on this occasion. 



If you’d like the opportunity to engage with students in Leeds and tell them more about the Foundation, just let me know and I can share the details. 



Many thanks, 

Neil Eggar --

subject: att. Jonny - London Universities - September
body_text:
Hi Jonny, 



Thanks for taking my call there. As mentioned, we work with universities across London to host their annual Freshers' Fairs each September. We also offer a suite of on-campus events and digital media options delivered on campuses specifically targeting the Gen Z student population. 



We are now preparing for Freshers 2026 at 10+ universities in London. These campuses collectively host around 150,000 students who are actively living, studying, and working in the city for the next 3 years. 



Dabbers is a great fit for this, especially if we can come up with something interactive. Pretty sure one of my colleagues spoke with a member of your team for this last year but it didn't happen - so hopefully this time around we'll find a way. 



Media on Freshers and digitals + case studies attached. 



Let me know if you're free for a Zoom or similar with me over next couple of weeks. 



Best, 

Matt Billington (he/him)

Hi Leona, 



Hope you are well! 



Thank you very much for your enquiry with Native and how we can help Forest Feast shine in front of students. 



Native is the UK’s largest student engagement platform. We connect brands like yours with 2.9 million students across 55+ university campuses through the UK’s biggest and most high-impact student events. 





Would you be free for an online meeting this week or early next week with our lovely campaign strategist? 



Look forward to speaking with you soon! 



Kind Regards 

Sheraaz Ahmed

Hi Ryan, 



I hope you’re well. 



We’ve had a cancellation for theFeel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness – 10th March 2026 | Location: The University of Leeds | Lifton Place: Union Square & Precinct - Union Square, Zone A | Start time: 11 am-5 pm: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qMgQIQmLNTuF9vOJjxmP5NUqRH9UeRnM/view?usp=sharing 



Since the space is now vacant, I can offer you a one-time offer for FREE. This is intended to give you the experience of the fair firsthand.

Package: This is an "Activate Lite" booking, which includes a table and a basic activation space (roller banners are not included). 



The Goal: It’s a brilliant way to test the waters, collect student feedback, and prep your strategy to drive even higher engagement at our future fairs. 

Because I can only offer this to one business, please let me know as soon as possible if you’d like to snap it up. I expect the space to be filled within the next hour.

We’ve had a cancellation for theFeel Good Fair: Sports & Fitness – 10th March 2026 | Location: The University of Leeds | Lifton Place: Union Square & Precinct - Union Square, Zone A | Start time: 11 am-5 pm: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qMgQIQmLNTuF9vOJjxmP5NUqRH9UeRnM/view?usp=sharing 



Since the space is now vacant, I can offer you a one-time offer for FREE. This is intended to give you the experience of the fair firsthand.

body_text:
Hey Giulia, 



Thanks for coming back! The event is this Saturday so we would need to move quite quickly - but we can organise you a space to come in and take a stall at the event in a primer location. 



Ideally, we'd love you to come in and give away slices of pizza, you can organise this in return for students following you guys on social media or signing up to your mailing list. You can also direct students to your venues across London, and use the opportunity to recruit students into jobs as well. 



If you wanted to use the day to film some soical content (e.g. of you interacting with students, or maybe students doing blind taste tests and guessing the pizza flavour) - this is also possible. 



A standard stall here is normally £1500 + VAT - but we can organise this for you for £500 + VAT (which includes stall set up, and access to the 8000 students) as it's so last-minute. 



If you want to discuss this, feel free to let me know a time that's good for you and I can give you a call. 



Thanks, 



Jack Deputy Sales Manager - Campus Advertising - 0204 587 9852

subject: Apple - the go-to tech brand throughout the academic journey?
body_text:
Hi Philip, 

I’m getting in touch to explore how we could help support Apple deepen its connection with Gen Z audiences across UK university campuses this year. 



At Native, we specialise in campus media, with direct access to 2.9 million students across 120 UK universities. It’s a highly influential, digitally native audience that values performance and creativity. 



From MacBooks and iPads powering coursework and content creation, to AirPods and accessories embedded in everyday student life, there’s a clear opportunity to position Apple as the go-to tech brand throughout the academic journey. 



We deliver integrated campus campaigns spanning experiential activations, student fairs (including 400+ Freshers’ and themed events annually), targeted email, social, digital display, and DOOH keeping brands visible at key decision-making moments across the academic year. 



We are delivering a substantial Brand Ambassador programme for Samsung currently, which is seeing incredible results. 



I’d love to explore how we could build a tailored, insight-led campus strategy that drives student uptake ahead of the new academic cycle. 



Would you be open to a 20–30 minute conversation next week to discuss what this could look like? 



Best, 
Tristan 



Tristan Taylor 

Brand Partnerships Director

subject: Student Fairs at University of Leeds
body_text:
Hi Lisa, 



I hope you’re well. 



We partner with Student Unions all across the UK, and we run the on-campus events at University of Leeds. Our students have shown a great interest in hair & beauty this year, so we wanted to see if you’re currently trying to attract more students and whether you’d be interested in attending. 



We have thousands of students confirmed to attend our Freshers Fair events coming up in September, and I think you would be a perfect fit, as this kind of presence tends to work extremely well. 



You will have the opportunity to step foot onto the university campus and meet your future customers face to face in their personal setting, which is a great way to build strong relationships and new clientele. 



Freshers Week is a key decision-making moment for students. It’s when their shopping habits form, so having a presence at Freshers puts your brand in front of thousands of students at exactly the right time. 



Please let me know your thoughts regarding the Freshers fair, and if you're interested, I’d be happy to share dates, locations, footfall figures and package options. 



We also have our University of Leeds: 'Feel Good' fair coming up on 10th March 2026. This would be a great way for you to test the waters and see just how beneficial attending our fairs would be. On this occasion we are happy to offer this fair to you for free. We would need confirmation of your attendance to this fair by no later than this Friday 6th March. 



Please let me know if you're happy to attend, and if you have any questions, please do not hesitate to ask. 



Kind regards Shiden Mezghebe - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01382 755 749

Matt.

Subject: Secure Freshers 2026 at University of Leeds – Early Rate Ending Soon

Good morning Olympia,

Thank you again for your time this morning. It was great to speak with you.

As mentioned, we’re currently taking bookings for Freshers 2026 at the University of Leeds, taking place this September. This is the largest student-facing event of the year, welcoming over 12,000 students per day, all actively looking for places to socialise, eat, drink and spend as they begin term.

Freshers represent a huge opportunity because:


  *
Students form habits in Week 1 — the venues they try first often become their go-to for the next 3+ years.
  *
It drives immediate footfall — exclusive offers and activations convert directly into bookings that same week.
  *
It builds long-term brand awareness — especially important for venues slightly outside the city-centre “default” locations.

As BOX Headingley is based in LS6, Freshers is particularly valuable. Many student bars and restaurants in LS1 and LS2 are already well known to new students and, in some cases, have restrictions on-campus promotion. Freshers represent a huge opportunity because:


  *
Students form habits in Week 1 — the venues they try first often become their go-to for the next 3+ years.
  *
It drives immediate footfall — exclusive offers and activations convert directly into bookings that same week.
  *
It builds long-term brand awareness — especially important for venues slightly outside the city-centre “default” locations.
