Hi Zoe,

It was lovely to meet you earlier this week ( sorry we missed you, Liam!). Great to hear more about Represent and your plans for the year - if the 18-24 student demographic is who you want to target and convert, then Native can certainly help you do this! Below is a very brief overview of the basic products we discussed - if you can share any key dates and timelines, desired outcomes, budget range and preferred media mix... I can provide a more polished proposal.


When building and planning for experiential activations (if and when you might choose to do this with us), we will supply creative ideation and present rationale during a collaborative pitch session using a bespoke deck and proposition.


Here is a quick breakdown of costs and what to expect from a campaign working with us…


EMAIL:

1st Party Opted-in Solus Emails - Can be regionally and demographically targeted within the 18-24 year old university student data set - with over 350,000 individual email addresses opted-in to receive communications from our trusted parters. Average open rate is 30%.

COST: £100 per 1000 targeted emails sent.


TARGETED PAID SOCIAL MEDIA ADS - SOCAIL+

Blends our creative + student-first messaging distributed through performance-focused paid social on behalf of our university partners. COST: £6,000 per 700,000 Impressions


FAIR EVENT CALENDAR:

LINK TO FAIR EVENTS CALANDAR HERE<https://www.native.fm/uk/fair-calendar> - Expect an average of 1000 attendees per location per fair - we can select a campus or multiple to suit - based on region. (we can supply all thats needed - including staff)

COST: £2,000 Per Fair Activation


NATIVE - CLIENT CASE STUDIES:

EXPLORE VIA THE LINK HERE<https://www.native.fm/uk/resources?type=Case+Study>


I know you were also keen to explore OOH opportunities. If that’s a route you’d like to go down, we can work with you to produce unique out-of-home media placements and experiences across campuses, we will follow up separately with examples and suggestions.


I've attached a list of universities we cover that are exclusive to Native. If there are any specific areas you'd like to explore, do let us know! As mentioned on the call, we also have relationships with the other small percentage of universities, and whilst non-exclusive, we work with them regularly. We're also expanding our coverage in the US and are now partnered with UCLA, this might work well with the US store location. We can give further details on that if needed.


Finally, I’d like to introduce Ryan. We’ve had a slight team change on our side, and Ryan will be coming on board to help with creative and commercial alignment, alongside me, who will lead the creative and experiential strategy.


If you feel I have missed anything, please do let me know - This is an exciting one, really looking forward to putting a tailored proposal together for you to meet your objectives!


All the best and speak soon,

Leah

subject: Reaching UK students on campus with Netflix
body_text:
Hi Marine,

University is where students define how they unwind and watch together, not
just what they study. The platforms that become part of shared living, late
nights, and downtime often stick long after graduation.

Netflix already fits how Gen Z watches - on-demand, mobile-first, and built
around shared moments in halls and shared flats, from binge-worthy series
to cultural moments everyone’s talking about. The opportunity is showing up
while those viewing habits and brand preferences are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9M UK students through on-campus and digital
activations, helping brands become the first choice before routines are set.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore this further?

Best,
Alex

-- 

Alex Stoffel

this weekend, you will be arriving Saturday, is that correct?

Many thanks,
Beth

subject: Brand New Activation Space 🙌
body_text:
** Brand New Space!
------------------------------------------------------------

We’re excited to introduce a brand-new activation opportunity, giving brands direct access to outdoor promotional space within student halls of residence.

Located at Talybont Court, the University’s largest accommodation site, this space offers strong daily footfall from students heading to and from lectures, creating an ideal environment for high-impact engagement.

To celebrate the launch, we’re offering an exclusive introductory rate:

• First four bookings available for just £600 + VAT 🌟
Bookings must be confirmed by 20th March and completed by Friday 12th June.

Limited availability — get in touch to secure a slot or find out more.

subject: Reaching Students at UK campuses
body_text:
Hello Catherine,
I hope you are having a great day!
I am reaching out as we have recently concluded another bumper Refreshers season and Mitchells & Butlers was identified by our students as a brand they would love to see on campus.
We had some great successes in 2025 welcoming multiple  bars and craft beer brands onto campuses.

Have you explored student marketing before? It would be great to have a  chat with you so do let me know if getting in front of 2.8 million students is a priority for Mitchells & Butlers.
Best wishes

Chris McEwan (he/him)

Regional Sales Manager
07515790023


Powering campus culture


Learn more about our Campus Media and Marketing services.


native.fm | LinkedIn

Office address: Albert House, 256-260 Old Street, London, EC1V 9DD. Registered Office: Pavilion View, Brighton, BN1 1EY. Registered as a company in England and Wales, number: 09729773. VAT Registration Number: 255631209.


Original Thread:
If you don't want to hear from me again, please let me know.

subject: Re: Thursday @ The BSLI
body_text:
Hi Zac,

Thank you so much for your email, that’s really kind of you.

Just so I’m clear, what would you need from us specifically on this? I’m on
my way to London now, and unfortunately we wouldn’t have the turnaround
time to produce any printables, signage or organise staffing at this stage.
It’s frustrating as it’s a great opportunity, but timings are just very
tight our side.

However maybe I am not understanding the need from our side.

Let me know your thoughts.

Kindest,
Emma

*Emma-Louise Maw* / Global Head of Partnerships

ThursdayThursday HQ, 30 Dukes Place, London, EC3A 7LP

> Hey Emma,
>
> Thanks for getting back to me, any fun plans for the weekend?
>
> Funny timing actually. As your email came in, I was on a call with the
> team and brought this up. I literally asked, *“How do we get this brand
> there?”*
>
> You’re a brand we really like and genuinely want to work with. You’d look
> great at The BSLI, and I’m confident you’d be happy with both the event and
> the audience.
>
> Long story short, *we can make this work.*
>
> If you’re able to have the internal conversation Monday or Tuesday, we can
> then maybe jump on a call, iron out the details, and get everything signed
> off quickly. If that happens, we’ll commit to making this happen on our
> side. We’ll absorb the time pressure and figure out the logistics, even if
> that means adjusting production or switching suppliers.
>
> And, if you have any physical assets from previous events that you think
> would work well, you’re more than welcome to bring those too!
>
> Honestly, to echo, this is the perfect place for Thursday to start. It’s a
> highly receptive audience, in a fun, no pressure environment, and a great
> way to test how students respond to something new and different in the
> dating space.
>
> Perhaps we should a call in on Monday, with both teams, to discuss this
> further?
>
> And on your point around our partners who could benefit from your
> audience, *we'll absolutely explore that!* I’ve asked to block some time
> next week with the team to look at it properly and will circle back
>
> To summarise, *we’d genuinely love to have you there and we’re committed
> to making it work.*
>
> Thanks & speak soon! Have a great weekend 🥂
> Zac

>>> I asked the team for info on "dating" within our audience, and can share
>>> the following:
>>>
>>> *1.* Only 29% state they currently go on dates. 40% not right now & 25%
>>> never.
>>>
>>> *2.* Of those who go on dates, top three motivating factors:
>>>
>>>    - Love - 62%
>>>    - Fun - 56%
>>>    - Companionship - 51%
>>>
>>> *3.* Of those who go on dates, 75% are looking for a serious
>>> relationship
>>>
>>> *4.* Top three factors that put people off dating:
>>>
>>>    - Can’t find people I like  - 36%
>>>    - Not interested right now- 26%
>>>    - Confidence issues - 22%
>>>
>>> Specifically regarding "dating apps & sites"
>>>
>>> *5.* Only 16% of respondents state that they use dating apps at the
>>> moment.
>>>
>>> *6.* However, of those who do, 41% state that they are using them more
>>> frequently compared to last year
>>>
>>> Long story short, the data shows a clear gap in the student market! Most
>>> students aren’t actively dating or using apps, but that doesn’t mean
>>> they’re disengaged; it points to dissatisfaction and untapped demand.
>>>
>>> Those who are dating are largely motivated by meaningful connection, and
>>> app/site usage among current users is increasing year-on-year.
>>>
>>> A sizable, under-penetrated audience of students wants genuine
>>> relationships but isn't being effectively served by existing platforms.
>>> This creates a strong opportunity for Thursday to position itself as the
>>> alternative!
>>>
>>> Let me know how you would like to move forward, or you need further
>>> clarification, or another call, etc.
>>> Zac

>>>>>>>>>>>> decided to pause on the current campaign we proposed, but if you’re open to
>>>>>>>>>>>> a quick chat, Pri and I would love to throw a few ideas your way,
>>>>>>>>>>>> specifically for this event?
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> I’ve attached a one-pager for context, but as a quick potential
>>>>>>>>>>>> idea, it could be fun to create a branded area that encourages and
>>>>>>>>>>>> incentivises students to take part in a shared activity, either with
>>>>>>>>>>>> friends or someone they’ve just met! For example, a live painting concept
>>>>>>>>>>>> where two people partner up and rotate every 30s, building a collaborative
>>>>>>>>>>>> piece over 5/10 mins.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> We’d handle the branding, brand ambassadors to drive
>>>>>>>>>>>> participation, and on-site engagement etc. There could also be a photo
>>>>>>>>>>>> moment so they leave with a memory of their “masterpiece” and anonymous
>>>>>>>>>>>> co-artist, along with leaflets, QRs etc.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Plenty of ways we could shape this, and it could work really
>>>>>>>>>>>> well as an intital smaller-scale test! Let me know your thoughts - we're
>>>>>>>>>>>> available anytime if you wanted to chat for 20 mins.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks & looking forward to hearing from you,

Hello Freya,
I hope you are having a great day!
I am reaching out as we have recently concluded another bumper Refreshers season and The Bierkeller was identified by our students as a brand they would love to see on campus.
We had some great successes in 2025 welcoming multiple  bars and craft beer brands onto campuses.

Have you explored student marketing before? It would be great to have a  chat with you so do let me know if getting in front of 2.8 million students is a priority for The Bierkeller.

subject: 2.9 million students across 120 UK universities
body_text:
Hi Sam, 



I’m getting in touch to explore how we could help support Apple deepen its connection with Gen Z audiences across UK university campuses this year. 



At Native, we specialise in campus media, with direct access to 2.9 million students across 120 UK universities. It’s a highly influential, digitally native audience that values performance and creativity. 



From MacBooks and iPads powering coursework and content creation, to AirPods and accessories embedded in everyday student life, there’s a clear opportunity to position Apple as the go-to tech brand throughout the academic journey. 



We deliver integrated campus campaigns spanning experiential activations, student fairs (including 400+ Freshers’ and themed events annually), targeted email, social, digital display, and DOOH keeping brands visible at key decision-making moments across the academic year. 



We are delivering a substantial Social Brand Ambassador programme for Samsung currently, which is seeing incredible results. 



I’d love to explore how we could build a tailored, insight-led campus strategy that drives student uptake ahead of the new academic cycle. 



Would you be open to a 20–30 minute conversation next week to discuss what this could look like? 



Best, 
Tristan 



Tristan Taylor 

Brand Partnerships Director

subject: Apple - the go-to tech brand throughout the academic journey?
body_text:
Hi Issac, 



I’m getting in touch to explore how we could help support Apple deepen its connection with Gen Z audiences across UK university campuses this year. 



At Native, we specialise in campus media, with direct access to 2.9 million students across 120 UK universities. It’s a highly influential, digitally native audience that values performance and creativity. 



From MacBooks and iPads powering coursework and content creation, to AirPods and accessories embedded in everyday student life, there’s a clear opportunity to position Apple as the go-to tech brand throughout the academic journey. 



We deliver integrated campus campaigns spanning experiential activations, student fairs (including 400+ Freshers’ and themed events annually), targeted email, social, digital display, and DOOH keeping brands visible at key decision-making moments across the academic year. 



We are delivering a substantial Brand Ambassador programme for Samsung currently, which is seeing incredible results. 



I’d love to explore how we could build a tailored, insight-led campus strategy that drives student uptake ahead of the new academic cycle. 



Would you be open to a 20–30 minute conversation next week to discuss what this could look like? 



Best, 
Tristan 



Tristan Taylor 

Brand Partnerships Director

subject: Reaching UK students on campus with Dropbox
body_text:
Hi Janina,

University is often when students define how they organise their work -
collaborating on group projects, storing notes in the cloud, and managing
files across multiple devices.

Dropbox already fits how digitally native Gen Z works - simple,
collaborative cloud storage designed for flexibility and productivity. The
opportunity is showing up while those workflow habits and platform
preferences are being formed.

At Native, we connect 2.9 million UK university students through on-campus
and digital activations, helping software brands build early adoption and
long-term loyalty.

Would you be open to a quick call this or next week to explore?
